item.title: Confused
item.content:encoded:
During the test with 956, it says that it had pink hair while wearing the mask. I understand that that the masks don't usually negate anomalous "powers" but it does change appearance to be as human as possible. What I'm getting at is, if the masks change the anomaly so it looks as normal as possible, wouldn't then the test log say that 956's was dyed pink, instead of it being "natural"?

Or was the pink haired girl just a reference to Miss Sweetie? (If this is the case then it wouldn't surprise that Wondertainment would make the masks turn the anomalies into people of similar characteristics to the misters if they were to have "matching themes")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote. A great hook i enjoy are crosslinks/testing! Helps new readers get familiar with different types of SCPs, and gives a good reason for crosstesting, instead of "lets see what would happen for shits and giggles" cliche

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fun concept and my favorite GOI. I also like that you explored a more caring side to wondertainment. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With all honesty though, we make these products because many don’t get the chance to experience a proper childhood, or even celebrate such a unique time of year. So during that magical time when the mundane blend with the norm, we want to celebrate it right and give a little more.

I love this part. It's sincere and shows how much he cares about everyone including the outcasts that must be contained. It is just so cool that Wondertainment made a product fully intended to make anomalies be normal in some way for once. The idea really invokes a few emotions so good job on that.

Upvoted.

item.title: Idea for scp 173 test log!
item.content:encoded:
I wrote a little test log for testing on SCP-173. I hope you like it! Feel free to use this if you want! I added a part on blood testing, but you can remove that if you don't like it. Hopefully I got all the details right. (You can edit this however you like. Change the mask type, ect.. Enjoy!)

Test: 3992-7-005
Subject: SCP-173
SCP-3992 Instance: SCP-3992-5 (Mummy mask)
Results: SCP-173 appeared as a bald athletic African human male. A pair of red pupils were visible through SCP-3992-5’s eyeholes. It no longer resembled a statue. SCP-173's was now wearing layers of yellow tinted linen cloth, wrapped around its entire body similar to an Egyptian mummy.

Notes: Of significant note, SCP-173’s anomalous properties ceased functioning while wearing SCP-3992-5. However, it remained standing still, breathing heavily, staring at the wall. Class-D personal were instructed to stare at it. Then, they were instructed to close their eyes simultaneously. As soon as they closed their eyes, SCP-173 ran up to them, and began snapping their necks one by one. He snapped them incredibly fast using his perceived human hands. He ran using his perceived human legs. Once all 3 test subjects were dead, SCP-173 stood still once more, and stared onward with a blank, empty stare. The mask was removed shortly after that test by Class D personnel. (The effects of SCP-3992 may prove useful for the containment of SCP-173’s anomalous properties. It slows down his attack speed tremendously.)

(Dr.█████ attempted to take blood from SCP-173 while it was still in human form, but no blood could be extracted from SCP-173. The needle went into the perceived artery, but no blood came out. Dr.█████ then proceeded to stab a scalpel into SCP-173's arm, but once again, no blood came out. No damage could be dealt to the humanoid creature, and it could not feel pain.) "Can we kill these SCP's once they are in human form?" (Dr.█████ then decided to take fingerprints from SCP-173 while in human form. No matching prints were found in the database.) Testing ended, and the mask was removed from SCP-173. SCP-3992-5 has been returned to its locker. Mask should be tested further.

Thanks for reading! I hope you can use this in your test logs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good concept, good GOI to explore that concept, but I felt like the execution could be better. Needs moar Aggressively Whimsical Catchphrases (tm).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gosh danged onion cutting ghost-ninjas. Just… take your +1. Too many feels to fight it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SECONDED. One of three things will happen, as far as I can figure:

1. 682 will turn into a harmless child, enjoy the experience, then go on an explosive rage-fueled death-bender (more than usual) for the indignity.

2. It will almost work, but then he'll adapt and use his newfound devil powers before being dumped back in the wash tub.

3. He won't change, but they won't be able to get it back from him because it's his now, dang it.

Either way, that'll be a fun romp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made a couple of spelling corrections… Affect/effect stuff

item.title: Re: Article suggestions
item.content:encoded: I think customizing the "disguise" effect to be relevant to the personality of the skip wearing it is a great idea - those ideas seem like they'd be tricky to come up with, but imo it would be a perfect way to make this stand on it's own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You found a picture!

Glad to see it's doing well, +1.

item.title: Article suggestions
item.content:encoded:
Thankyou for the feedback so far everyone.

Taking the most common piece of feedback into account, I'd like to share a few ideas I have for changes to add to SCP-3992.

I was thinking of featuring in the testing logs a few anomalous humanoids (not SCP's, simply people in Foundation custody) as well as a few SCP-████'s. To make the logs less crosslinky.

As well as this, I was thinking of giving the masks the ability to take the wearers personal preference into account, and change the costume they appear dressed in to their personal idea of what that monster would look like.

Thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
holding off on voting, i want to give it a -1 for above reasons. but this is just too damn cute and written well i don't have the heart to.

plus i love wondertainment

but i don't want to +1 either.
so.
i won't save you but i won't bring this down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it enough to give a +1, but seeing the phrase "To clarify" in an article rubs me the wrong way for some reason. However, that's just nitpicking and that didn't take away from my overall enjoyment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay for Halloween Wondertainment stuff! And with a healthy side of cross-testing too! +1~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cross-testing! Glorious, glorious gratuitous cross-testing in series IV! It is a little more justified than in series I skips since the masks only work on anomalies, but I love it. You get an upvote.

Edit: Let's try it on 682!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a very understandable option. I did want to feature more than just SCP's in the testing logs but had trouble coming up with a scenario for how the Foundation could test it the mass on physically abnormal children.

I didn't want to use people with mundane abnormalities though, as Wondertainment wouldn't want to come off as being insensitive to those with such appearances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is cute, it's written well (though you have a few grammar errors that could have been caught with more proofreading). It tells a little bit of a story - it's a simple one, but this is a perspective on Wondertainment I haven't seen before.

What keeps me at neutral is, as DrSevere pointed out, that this doesn't stand alone. A test log totally reliant on cross-links isn't an idea I'm a fan of, especially when the tests are basically the same thing happening the same way in each one. In a larger sense, though, I feel like most of the backstory/context of this is reliant on the relationship between Wondertainment and the foundation/the foundation and anomalies. This is a matter of personal taste, but I prefer skips that are about the Foundation encountering things in the world, rather than centered on the Foundation itself, and I feel like this is firmly in the latter category.

Still, it's a nice read, provoked a couple thoughts, and definitely is in the spirit of Halloween.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea of Wondertainment creating disguises for their other anomalies, but this is one of those SCPs that is totally reliant on crosslinking for a hook, something I'm just not a fan of. I'd rather see this be an interesting standalone and then have a separate supplemental testing log, rather than have the test log be the focus of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Happy Halloween everyone!

Decided to write a Wondertainment skip for the holiday.

Credits:
Image from Maskworld.com and can be found here (Permission granted)

Many thanks to Leveritas for their feedback, much appreciated.
